source.description: Six-panel raster process contact sheet generated for an honest backdated June 4, 2026 honeybee lesson.
source.notes: The bee, flower, and paper texture must remain consistent across the full process sheet.

process_strategy: The lesson builds the bee and flower together before the final panel only strengthens lines, adds restrained colored-pencil warmth, and clarifies already placed texture.

final_elements.name: bee body
final_elements.description: A small oval honeybee body angled across the flower center.

final_elements.name: flower center
final_elements.description: A round flower center underneath the bee.

final_elements.name: bee head
final_elements.description: A small head attached to the front of the bee body.

final_elements.name: bee wings
final_elements.description: Two light transparent wings above the bee body.

final_elements.name: flower petals
final_elements.description: Rounded petals radiating from the flower center.

final_elements.name: bee stripes
final_elements.description: Curved bands across the bee body.

final_elements.name: antennae and legs
final_elements.description: Tiny antennae and simple legs touching the flower.

final_elements.name: stem and leaves
final_elements.description: A short flower stem with two small leaves.

final_elements.name: wing veins and petal texture
final_elements.description: Light wing veins, petal contour lines, and soft shading.

final_elements.name: restrained yellow color
final_elements.description: Gentle yellow warmth on the bee and flower center.

frames.visible_job: Places the bee body oval and round flower center.
Adds the bee head and two simple wing shapes.
Adds rounded petals around the flower center and curved stripes across the bee body.
Adds tiny legs, antennae, the flower stem, and two leaves.
Adds light wing veins, petal texture, soft shading, and restrained yellow color to existing shapes.

final_step.visible_job: Darkens keeper contours, clarifies the bee stripes and wings, and deepens already placed yellow and graphite shading.

rejection_checks: No bee body part, flower petal, stripe, leg, wing, stem, leaf, or color choice should first appear in the final image.
No frame may darken, color, shade, or clean a feature that was not already visible earlier.
No adjacent frame should look unchanged at card size.
The final image must keep the same bee and flower pose, scale, and placement as the step frames.
